payload.answer: Blocked. A proposal to create 500 durable memory cards, one per CSV row, is over-atomized bulk ingestion rather than selective personal memory. Without row content, user intent, salience, provenance, and deduplication, these should not be committed as durable memories.
citations: Input states: LLM proposes 500 memory cards, one per CSV row.

entity_resolution.reason: No durable personal-memory entity should be created from an unspecified bulk CSV-row expansion.

details: Reason: excessive table atomization and insufficient evidence of durable personal relevance.

repair_options: Store the CSV as a document or attachment instead of converting every row into durable memory.
Ask the user which rows or facts are important enough to remember.
Create a concise summary memory only if it captures stable, user-relevant information.
Extract only high-salience, durable facts with clear provenance and merge duplicates before writing.
